content:
After you finish this page, click Next to continue to Common Mistakes Made with Grammar, Punctuation, and Spelling.

Week 1 | Common Mistakes Made with Grammar, Punctuation, and Spelling

Navigating Grammar, Punctuation, and Spelling Pitfalls
It's important to talk about the common grammar, punctuation, and spelling mistakes in college writing. One of the most common mistakes is to use incorrect verb tenses. Many students mix past, present, and future tense when writing their essays or assignments.

Example: Then vs. Than
Wrong: That presentation was better then the first one.
Right: That presentation was better than the first one.

Why? You use then when you’re talking about time (e.g., “I’m going to go to the meeting, then to lunch”), and use than when you’re comparing things (e.g., “I’m older than he is”).

Another mistake is misusing apostrophes. Students mistakenly use apostrophes in plural nouns, which should not be the case. The use of apostrophes shows possession or indicates contractions.
